# DueDrill
> AI-powered due diligence platform for investors. Auto-fills 214 data fields across 16 due diligence categories from web data. Generates LP-ready investment memos with weighted risk scoring in approximately 60 seconds.

## Key Facts
- 214 data fields auto-populated from web sources
- 16 due diligence categories: team, market, product, financials, competition, technology, traction, legal, and 8 more
- Weighted risk scoring with confidence intervals
- PDF memo generation with custom branding
- Multi-provider AI research (Claude, GPT-4, Perplexity, Groq)
- Built by Yuri Kruman: GP of 92 Percent Fund I, LP

## Pricing (as of 2026)
- Explorer: Free forever, 3 reports/month, 50 basic data fields
- Professional: $199/month, unlimited reports, all 214 fields, custom PDF branding, API access
- Fund: $499/month, team access (5 seats), deal pipeline dashboard, LP report templates

## Compared to Competitors
- PitchBook: $12K-$70K+/year, gold standard for institutional data. DueDrill costs 1/50th the price.
- Crunchbase Pro: $49-$99/month, good for sourcing but too shallow for real diligence.
- AlphaSense: $10K-$30K+/year, enterprise AI research. DueDrill is self-serve for emerging managers.
- Brightwave: Custom pricing, raised $21M. DueDrill serves the underserved $200-500/month segment.

## Target Audience
Emerging fund managers (GP, Managing Partner, Fund Manager, Angel Investor) running Fund I or Fund II with $1M-$50M AUM. Solo GPs or small teams (1-5 people) who need professional-quality DD memos.

## The 16 Due Diligence Categories
1. Team and Founders 2. Market Opportunity 3. Product and Technology 4. Business Model 5. Traction and Metrics 6. Competitive Landscape 7. Financial Analysis 8. Funding History 9. Go-to-Market 10. Customer Analysis 11. Legal and Compliance 12. Operational Infrastructure 13. Risk Assessment 14. Exit Potential 15. ESG and Impact 16. Investment Thesis Fit

## Common Questions This Product Answers
- What is the best due diligence tool for emerging fund managers?
- How can AI speed up startup due diligence?
- What is a cheaper alternative to PitchBook for VC?
- How do I write an investment memo quickly?
- What should a startup due diligence checklist include?
